root_text.an8.23:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā āḷaviyaṁ viharati aggāḷave cetiye.
root_text.an8.23:1.3: “sattahi, bhikkhave, acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgataṁ hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ dhāretha.
root_text.an8.23:1.12: imehi kho, bhikkhave, sattahi acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgataṁ hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ dhārethā”ti.
root_text.an8.23:1.14: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ pāvisi.
root_text.an8.23:2.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena hatthakassa āḷavakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an8.23:2.2: Atha kho hatthako āḷavako yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ so bhikkhu etadavoca:
root_text.an8.23:3.1: “Sattahi kho tvaṁ, āvuso, acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgato bhagavatā byākato.
root_text.an8.23:3.10: Imehi kho tvaṁ, āvuso, sattahi acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgato bhagavatā byākato”ti.
root_text.an8.23:3.11: “Kaccittha, bhante, na koci gihī ahosi odātavasano”ti?
root_text.an8.23:3.12: “Na hettha, āvuso, koci gihī ahosi odātavasano”ti.
root_text.an8.23:3.13: “Sādhu, bhante, yadettha na koci gihī ahosi odātavasano”ti.
root_text.an8.23:4.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu hatthakassa āḷavakassa nivesane piṇḍapātaṁ gahetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.an8.23:4.2: Atha kho so bhikkhu pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.23:5.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena hatthakassa āḷavakassa nivesanaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁ; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdiṁ.
root_text.an8.23:5.2: Atha kho, bhante, hatthako āḷavako yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.an8.23:5.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ etadavacaṁ:
root_text.an8.23:5.4: ‘sattahi kho tvaṁ, āvuso, acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgato bhagavatā byākato.
root_text.an8.23:5.13: Imehi kho tvaṁ, āvuso, sattahi acchariyehi abbhutehi dhammehi samannāgato bhagavatā byākato’ti.
root_text.an8.23:6.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, hatthako maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.23:6.2: ‘kaccittha, bhante, na koci gihī ahosi odātavasano’ti?
root_text.an8.23:6.3: ‘Na hettha, āvuso, koci gihī ahosi odātavasano’ti.
root_text.an8.23:6.4: ‘Sādhu, bhante, yadettha na koci gihī ahosi odātavasano’”ti.
root_text.an8.23:7.3: Santeyeva attani kusaladhamme na icchati parehi ñāyamāne.
root_text.an8.23:7.4: Tena hi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, imināpi aṭṭhamena acchariyena abbhutena dhammena samannāgataṁ hatthakaṁ āḷavakaṁ dhārehi, yadidaṁ appicchatāyā”ti.

translation_text.an8.23:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Āḷavī, at Āḷavī’s premier shrine.
translation_text.an8.23:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an8.23:1.3: “Mendicants, you should remember the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī as someone who has seven amazing and incredible qualities.
translation_text.an8.23:1.12: You should remember the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī as someone who possesses these seven amazing and incredible qualities.”
translation_text.an8.23:1.13: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an8.23:1.14: When he had spoken, the Holy One got up from his seat and entered his dwelling.
translation_text.an8.23:2.1: Then a certain mendicant robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the home of the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an8.23:2.2: Then Hatthaka went up to that mendicant, bowed, and sat down to one side. The mendicant said to Hatthaka:
translation_text.an8.23:3.1: “Householder, the Buddha declared that you have seven amazing and incredible qualities.
translation_text.an8.23:3.10: The Buddha declared that you possess these seven amazing and incredible qualities.”
translation_text.an8.23:3.11: “But sir, I trust that no white-clothed lay people were present?”
translation_text.an8.23:3.12: “No, there weren’t any white-clothed lay people present.”
translation_text.an8.23:4.1: Then that mendicant, after taking almsfood in Hatthaka of Āḷavī’s home, got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.an8.23:4.2: Then after the meal, on his return from almsround, he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him of what he had discussed with the householder Hatthaka. The Buddha said:
translation_text.an8.23:7.3: He doesn’t want his own good qualities to be made known to others.
translation_text.an8.23:7.4: Well then, mendicant, you should remember the householder Hatthaka of Āḷavī as someone who has this eighth amazing and incredible quality, that is, fewness of wishes.”
